message:
fix(packs/core/git): push-force-long no longer false-matches `--force` in branch names

`push-force-long` used `.*--force(?![-a-z])` which greedily matched
`--force` anywhere in the push arg list. A branch name like
`feature--force` (unusual but valid) would trigger the Critical rule:

  git push origin feature--force    -> false-matched push-force-long

Switched to the token-walker `(?:\S+\s+)*--force(?![-a-z])` consistent
with push-force-short. The walker steps through space-separated args
one at a time and never reaches `--force` substrings INSIDE a single
token.

Legitimate `git push --force`, `git push --force origin main`, and the
safer `--force-with-lease` negative-lookahead carve-out are unchanged.
